mnemonics.title: Power Formula Remember
mnemonics.content: Power = Capacitance × Voltage² × Frequency → 'Clever Vipers Find Food' (C V² F)

mnemonics.title: Energy Optimization Techniques
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'DPC' - DVFS, Power Capping, C-states (CPU idle states)

mnemonics.title: Green IT Goals
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'GREEN' - Reduce Energy, Extend lifecycle, Enable sustainability, Notify users, Nurture environment

acronyms.usage: Energy-saving technique that adjusts CPU voltage and frequency based on workload
Maximum heat generated by a processor that cooling system must dissipate
Sector responsible for 2-4% of global carbon emissions
Strategy to complete tasks quickly to reach low-power idle state faster
Power saving states (C0, C1, C2, etc.) used during CPU idle management

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a triangle: P at top, C at bottom-left, V² at bottom-right, f at bottom-center. P = C × V² × f shows power increases exponentially with voltage (squared effect)
Think of a water tank: Power is the pipe diameter (flow rate), Energy is the total water collected. Same pipe (power) but running longer gives more water (energy)
Picture a staircase: Each step represents a frequency/voltage level. Moving down the staircase during light work reduces energy. More steps = more granular control
Imagine a leaky faucet (static/always leaking) vs water running only when needed (dynamic). Both contribute to water (energy) waste

analogies.analogy: Like a car engine consuming fuel not just when driving (runtime) but not when parked (manufacturing/disposal)
A light bulb in a room - at 10% brightness using only 1% energy (proportional) vs old bulbs using 50% energy even at dim settings (not proportional)
Cleaning a room quickly at full speed vs slowly while taking breaks - finishing fast lets you relax (idle) longer, saving overall energy
Like driving a car - using less gas (voltage) and slower speed (frequency) when traffic is light, full speed on highways
